cards.title: E3 Framework Cuts LLM Agent Token Use by 91% via Complexity-Aware Execution
cards.why_it_matters: As an AI/research engineer building or evaluating LLM agents, this directly addresses the compute waste from over-reading context, with concrete metrics and a released benchmark to evaluate against.
cards.summary: E3 (Estimate, Execute, Expand) gives LLM agents a complexity-awareness step before acting: estimate minimum required scope, execute that path, expand only on failure. On MSE-Bench (121 deterministic edits), E3 matches 100% task success while cutting cost 85%, tokens 91%, and inspected files 92% vs. strongest baselines. Validated on a live GPT-4o agent editing real open-source code via pytest.

cards.title: Seriality Gap: Video Diffusion Models Structurally Can't Scale Serial Computation for Causal Chains
cards.why_it_matters: If you work on world models or video generation for simulation/reasoning, this is a fundamental architectural finding showing that adding denoising steps does not provide the serial compute needed for dependent-event reasoning.
cards.summary: Controlled experiments on multi-ball hard-sphere dynamics show bidirectional video diffusion degrades as causal chains lengthen, but not in length-matched single-ball controls, isolating dependent-event structure as the cause. The authors prove that denoising steps add no serial computation beyond the backbone. Autoregressive/blockwise generation and greater architectural depth improve performance, pointing toward serial-compute-aware architectures.

cards.title: TerraZero: RL-Only Autonomous Driving Agent Tops InterPlan Benchmark at 1.3M Steps/Second
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers working on RL, agents, or simulation infrastructure, this is a strong result showing zero-demonstration RL generalizing across cities and reaching SOTA on a long-tail driving benchmark without a fallback planner.
cards.summary: TerraZero is a procedural driving simulator and self-play RL stack achieving 1.3M agent-steps/second on a single server GPU via a zero-copy CPU/GPU path. Policies train from scratch with no human demonstrations and zero-shot generalize across cities, including emergent left-hand traffic. First fully learned policy to top the InterPlan long-tail benchmark; posts best collision and time-to-collision scores on val14; competitive with reference-anchored self-play on Waymo realism.

cards.title: Discrete-Diffusion ASR: Frozen DiffusionGemma 26B MoE Reaches 6.6% WER on LibriSpeech in ~8 Parallel Steps
cards.why_it_matters: For researchers in speech, diffusion LMs, or efficient inference, this demonstrates that a frozen discrete-diffusion backbone can be audio-adapted with only 0.16% of parameters trained, and that a CTC loss is necessary to ground audio in the attention pathway.
cards.summary: A frozen Whisper encoder feeds into a lightweight projector and LoRA adapters (~42M trainable parameters, 0.16% of backbone) to give DiffusionGemma—a 26B MoE discrete-diffusion LM using uniform random-token diffusion—ASR capability. Standard training objectives fail because audio gradients don't reach the projector; a CTC loss applied through the frozen output head fixes this. Achieves 6.6% WER on LibriSpeech test-clean in roughly eight parallel denoising steps across utterance lengths.

cards.title: PalmClaw: On-Device Native LLM Agent Framework for Mobile Phones with 11.5% Task Success Gain
cards.why_it_matters: For engineers building agentic systems, this shows a concrete alternative to GUI-scraping mobile agents by exposing device capabilities as structured tools with explicit execution boundaries, with strong benchmarked improvements.
cards.summary: PalmClaw is an open-source agent framework running natively on mobile, managing sessions, memory, skills, and tool calls directly on-device. It exposes device capabilities as structured tools with explicit arguments and defined execution boundaries rather than GUI action sequences. Experiments show 11.5% relative improvement in task success and 94.9% reduction in completion time over the strongest baseline.

cards.title: GyroFlow: Flow Matching Bypasses Transient Simulation to Directly Sample Gyrokinetic Turbulence
cards.why_it_matters: Researchers interested in generative models for physical simulation will find this relevant as a principled application of latent flow matching to a 5D physics problem, with a new distributional evaluation metric and warm-start use case.
cards.summary: GyroFlow is a latent generative model that directly samples steady-state gyrokinetic turbulence statistics in 5D phase space under an ergodicity assumption, bypassing full transient simulation entirely. It outperforms autoregressive, reduced-order, and other generative baselines and can warm-start the numerical solver. Authors also introduce FGyD, a distributional quality metric in the latent space of a pretrained gyrokinetic model, correlated with downstream flux accuracy.
